title: Woman Of The Year

jokes.text: I would hardly call what he does coaching.
jokes.setup: Conflict between coaches, call for adult behavior
jokes.punchline: One coach immediately insults the other's coaching ability
jokes.explanation: Good immediate undermining of the call for civility, well-timed character moment

jokes.text: Wow. Excellent role modeling.
jokes.setup: Leslie just witnessed the coaches arguing like children
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic praise for their terrible behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie sarcasm, functional but predictable

jokes.text: Every child has the right to play, no matter how boring the sport.
jokes.setup: Leslie making an inspirational speech about children's rights
jokes.punchline: Casually insults soccer as boring in the same breath
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - passionate advocacy undercut by casual dismissiveness. Great economy and character work.

jokes.text: I'm the girls' coach. She's the boys' coach. Oh. Okay. Sorry. That's sexist.
jokes.setup: Leslie assumes gender roles incorrectly
jokes.punchline: Gets called out for her own sexist assumption
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Leslie the feminist advocate makes a sexist assumption. Well-structured reversal.

jokes.text: I'm a boy. Good. Yeah. 'Cause I wasn't talking to you.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to recover from her mistake with kids
jokes.punchline: Gets gender wrong again and dismisses a child awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leslie's awkwardness with gender assumptions

jokes.text: Jerry! You got soup all over our mail! You jerk.
jokes.setup: Mail arrives at the office
jokes.punchline: Jerry has somehow gotten soup on the mail, immediate hostility
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry incompetence moment, well-established character dynamic

jokes.text: I have been a dues-paying member since I was nine.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her connection to the women's organization
jokes.punchline: She joined a women's organization as a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie absurdity - of course she would join a women's org as a child. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: The IOW is proud to announce this year's Dorothy Everton Smythe Female Empowerment Award Winner, Ronald Swanson.
jokes.setup: Everyone expects Leslie to win the women's award
jokes.punchline: Ron Swanson, the most masculine man, wins instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant central premise - the most anti-feminist character winning a feminist award. Perfect setup for episode conflict.

jokes.text: They only honor women. And Ron's the opposite of a woman.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to process the impossible news
jokes.punchline: States the obvious about Ron's masculinity in crisis mode
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction, stating the obvious in a funny way

jokes.text: Well, it's about time.
jokes.setup: Ron learning he won a women's empowerment award
jokes.punchline: Completely unsurprised reaction, as if he deserved it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan - accepting a women's award like it's his due. Excellent economy and character work.

jokes.text: However, she cares way too much about crap like this, which is why I can't pass up this opportunity to tease her about it.
jokes.setup: Ron's talking head about declining the award
jokes.punchline: Admits he's keeping it just to mess with Leslie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Ron's mischievous side coming through

jokes.text: Hey, Freddy. Good news! Your liquor license renewal got got, yo.
jokes.setup: Tom delivering good news to Freddy
jokes.punchline: His awkward slang 'got got, yo' trying to sound cool
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom trying-too-hard moment with made-up slang

jokes.text: Maybe call it something like Club-a-Dub-Dub or The Clubmarine, sort of a submarine-themed club.
jokes.setup: Tom talking about opening his own club
jokes.punchline: Terrible club names, especially the submarine theme
jokes.explanation: Good Tom absurdity - the submarine-themed club is particularly ridiculous

jokes.text: Or Tom's Bistro. The word 'bistro' is classy as.
jokes.setup: Tom continuing to brainstorm club names
jokes.punchline: Thinks 'bistro' is inherently classy, cuts off his own sentence
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment - his pretentiousness and incomplete thoughts

jokes.text: I agree. That's why I got into public service. To help me.
jokes.setup: Freddy saying Tom would be a great asset with his connections
jokes.punchline: Tom admits he only got into public service for selfish reasons
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tom moment - accidentally honest about his selfishness. Great economy and character truth.

jokes.text: $10,000. That's chump change.
jokes.setup: Freddy quotes the investment amount
jokes.punchline: Tom acts like $10k is nothing when he clearly doesn't have it
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom overconfidence masking financial reality

jokes.text: Write an e-mail to Barack Obama. Lock myself in my office and scream until I lose my voice.
jokes.setup: Leslie making a list of what to do about the award situation
jokes.punchline: Completely disproportionate responses including contacting the President
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie crisis response - completely over the top solutions. Great character work.

jokes.text: No way. Yesterday, if you would have asked me, I would've said no, but thank God, my grandfather just died, so I am flush with cash!
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio hearing about the investment opportunity
jokes.punchline: Celebrates his grandfather's death for the inheritance money
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Jean-Ralphio moment - casually celebrating death for money. Perfect character writing.

jokes.text: Also, sorry about your grandpa. No worries. He was a dick.
jokes.setup: Tom offering condolences
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio dismisses it by insulting his dead grandfather
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the grandfather joke, perfect economy

jokes.text: I don't get anything until I'm 50, which is a waste, 'cause I'm gonna be a billionaire in Costa Rica by then, eating dolphins and hanging out with lady singers.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio explaining his trust fund situation
jokes.punchline: Bizarre future plans involving eating dolphins
jokes.explanation: Peak Jean-Ralphio absurdity - the dolphin eating is wonderfully random and specific

jokes.text: Also, The Bag of Smells was a fun experiment, but it has to stop.
jokes.setup: April listing complaints about Andy as a roommate
jokes.punchline: References some mysterious 'Bag of Smells' experiment
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird Andy project referenced casually. Great specificity and economy.

jokes.text: Come on! No. We're finally starting to get serious results.
jokes.setup: April wants to stop the Bag of Smells
jokes.punchline: Andy thinks his smell experiment is yielding scientific results
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Andy's sincere belief in his absurd project

jokes.text: That's not a working fireplace, Andy. Stop writing phone messages on the wall, Andy. Dude, seriously, I love you but stop digging that weird hole in the backyard.
jokes.setup: Andy complaining about his controlling roommate
jokes.punchline: Reveals increasingly bizarre behavior culminating in mysterious hole digging
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Andy's weird behavior - the hole digging is the perfect topper

jokes.text: Well, we're not Mouse Rat anymore. We changed it to Tackleshaft.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the band breaking up
jokes.punchline: Reveals they changed to an even more ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the band name running gag, Tackleshaft is appropriately terrible

jokes.text: April, you're like an angel with no wings. So, like, a person?
jokes.setup: Andy trying to give April a poetic compliment
jokes.punchline: April points out the logical flaw in his metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy/April dynamic - his sweet stupidity meets her logical deflation. Perfect structure and character work.

jokes.text: Really? You're saying a women's organization made a mistake?
jokes.setup: Leslie suggesting the women's org made a mistake
jokes.punchline: Ron sarcastically points out the irony of her statement
jokes.explanation: Good Ron moment catching Leslie in contradicting her feminist principles

jokes.text: Yes. Camp Xena. Athena. Camp Athena. You don't even know the name. I almost got it. I was pretty close. No.
jokes.setup: Ron trying to recall the name of Leslie's project
jokes.punchline: Gets it wrong, claims he was close when he wasn't
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ron's indifference to Leslie's work

jokes.text: Look, Leslie, your job, which you're great at, is making me look good. Which you've clearly done well because I'm getting an award. Right?
jokes.setup: Ron explaining the award situation
jokes.punchline: Credits Leslie with making him look good enough to win her award
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron logic - taking credit while acknowledging Leslie did the work. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Well, it's a man's world, Winston. Get used to it. I'm a man.
jokes.setup: Leslie being dismissive to a child about the field situation
jokes.punchline: Gets the child's gender wrong again
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Leslie's gender confusion with kids

jokes.text: Dress code? Black tie optional. Just like life.
jokes.setup: Tom describing his investment presentation details
jokes.punchline: Makes a pseudo-profound comparison between dress code and life
jokes.explanation: Good Tom pretentiousness - trying to sound deep with meaningless comparison

jokes.text: I'm doing my official portrait for the IUD Awards Dinner Program. IOW.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her portrait shoot
jokes.punchline: Gets the acronym wrong, calling it IUD instead of IOW
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism - IUD is funnier than IOW, shows her flustered state

jokes.text: Which of these objects most represents women for this portrait? A pot? Or this Deputy Director Barbie?
jokes.setup: Leslie asking for advice on props for her portrait
jokes.punchline: Choices between sexist stereotypes - domestic pot or career Barbie
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Leslie's obliviousness to gender stereotypes

jokes.text: I'm going to help my boss win an award.
jokes.setup: Leslie playing with the Barbie
jokes.punchline: Makes the Barbie say exactly what Leslie is doing - helping her boss win her award
jokes.explanation: Good meta-moment where Leslie unconsciously describes her situation through the Barbie

jokes.text: Everything I do is the attitude of an award winner because I've won an award.
jokes.setup: Leslie questioning Ron's attitude for an award winner
jokes.punchline: Ron's circular logic that everything he does is award-worthy because he won
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron logic - completely circular but delivered with total confidence. Great character moment.

jokes.text: I can afford, like, 300 bucks a month. I've always wanted a doorman named Ernie. That'd be awesome. Or Kipp. I'm pretty flexible on that.
jokes.setup: April asking about Andy's apartment preferences
jokes.punchline: Has specific doorman name preferences despite tiny budget
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - completely unrealistic expectations with oddly specific details. Great character work.

jokes.text: Here's a great one. It's a Tudor mansion. It's got seven rooms, four bathrooms. It's got a tennis court, a pool and a three-car garage. What? And it's only $20,000 a month. And it's in Chicago. Ah! That close! It was almost perfect.
jokes.setup: April reading apartment listings to Andy, describing luxury mansion
jokes.punchline: Andy gets excited until hearing the price and location, thinks it 'almost' worked
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline structure with perfect Andy optimism about completely impossible apartment

jokes.text: Ladies! Jay-Z. Rihanna. Audrina Patridge. Jon Gosselin. Lady Gaga. Snooki.
jokes.setup: Tom starting his investment presentation
jokes.punchline: Random celebrity name-dropping including reality TV stars
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom trying to impress with celebrity names - fairly cheap humor but in character

jokes.text: For the small price of only one, one, one, thousand, thousand, thousand dollars, dollars, dollars!
jokes.setup: Tom revealing the investment amount
jokes.punchline: Dramatic repetition like a game show host
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom overselling - the repetition is appropriately cheesy for his character

jokes.text: Mark, I'm talking to you! Donna, I'm talking to you! Jerry! I'm talking to you! Mark! I'm talking to you!
jokes.setup: Tom trying to get people interested
jokes.punchline: Desperately points at everyone, repeats Mark's name
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's desperation, the Mark repetition suggests his panic

jokes.text: I have the Dorothy Every Time Smurf Girl Trophy for excellence in female stuff.
jokes.setup: Leslie showing off her awards, Ron responding
jokes.punchline: Completely butchers the award name, dismisses it as 'female stuff'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron dismissiveness - mangles the prestigious award name and reduces it to 'female stuff'. Excellent character work.

jokes.text: Come on, Leslie, you know I'm not sexist. I love powerful women. You do attend a shocking number of WNBA games.
jokes.setup: Ron defending himself against sexism charges
jokes.punchline: Leslie's evidence is his surprising WNBA attendance
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Ron secretly attending women's basketball fits his contradictions perfectly

jokes.text: She was the first woman in Pawnee to wear pants on a Sunday. She spent four years in jail for that.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining Dorothy Everton Smythe's achievements
jokes.punchline: Absurd historical detail about pants-wearing being illegal on Sundays
jokes.explanation: Great absurd historical detail that feels believable for Pawnee's ridiculous past

jokes.text: The acoustics in the bathroom are insane.
jokes.setup: Andy and April looking at a potential apartment
jokes.punchline: Andy's priority is the bathroom acoustics
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment - focusing on weird details like bathroom acoustics

jokes.text: I could definitely picture myself doing something over there.
jokes.setup: Andy looking around the apartment
jokes.punchline: Vague statement about 'doing something' - no specific plans
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy vagueness - enthusiastic but completely non-specific

jokes.text: It screams Andy Dwyer.
jokes.setup: Andy assessing whether the apartment suits him
jokes.punchline: Thinks a terrible, smelly apartment 'screams' his name
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Andy has no standards and thinks a dump suits him

jokes.text: We could do the usual boring stuff like eat some chicken salad with some crackers, or watch the pay-per-view, or we could go to my nightclub and do some dancing.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to sell Mark on the investment using a scenario
jokes.punchline: Describes normal activities as boring, assumes ownership of club that doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Good Tom cringe - already calling it 'his' club before investing

jokes.text: You'd never have to beg for sex again. I don't wanna get into this, really, but I don't beg for sex now.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to sell the club benefits
jokes.punchline: Mark shuts down Tom's inappropriate sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Tom's inappropriate pitch meets Mark's reasonable boundary

jokes.text: Just to be clear, you wouldn't be an owner, per se. You, me and my boy, Jean-Ralphio, would each own part of a share.
jokes.setup: Donna expressing interest in being an owner
jokes.punchline: Tom reveals they'd be splitting ownership of just one share
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Tom's deceptive sales tactics revealed. Shows his desperation.

jokes.text: We didn't make a mistake. We made a very conscious decision this year to choose a man.
jokes.setup: Everyone assuming the women's org made an error
jokes.punchline: They deliberately chose a man for a women's award
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot twist - subverts expectations about the mistake, reveals deeper institutional issues

jokes.text: Well, every year, we give it to a woman, and, frankly, nobody cares.
jokes.setup: Elise explaining their decision
jokes.punchline: Admits women's achievements get no attention
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as comedy - points out real media bias

jokes.text: Well, we're giving it to you. So, you're gonna take it, like a man.
jokes.setup: Ron trying to decline the women's award
jokes.punchline: Told to accept it 'like a man' - ironic for a women's award
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic line - accepting a women's award 'like a man' perfectly captures the absurdity

jokes.text: The IOW is a bunch of sexist jerks who need to get back in the kitchen where they belong, and leave the real feminist work to actual feminists like Ron Swanson!
jokes.setup: Leslie getting increasingly angry at the women's organization
jokes.punchline: Calls Ron Swanson an 'actual feminist' in her rage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Leslie's feminist rage leads her to call the most anti-feminist character a feminist. Perfect absurdity.

jokes.text: I won't. I'm gonna push my punches as far as my fists can extend!
jokes.setup: Leslie promising not to pull punches in the speech
jokes.punchline: Mangles the idiom about extending punches with fists
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie malapropism - trying to sound tough but mixing up the metaphor

jokes.text: Step three, I dagger you on the dance floor. Just bounce, bounce, bounce, bounce Now, all the ladies say it Bounce, bounce, bounce, bounce, bounce
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio explaining his club plan to Donna
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate dancing/sexual reference with terrible rapping
jokes.explanation: Classic Jean-Ralphio inappropriate behavior - the terrible rapping is very in character

jokes.text: I'm out. Why? I hate that guy.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio's terrible sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Donna immediately bails, honest about hating Jean-Ralphio
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna response - no filter, completely honest reaction to Jean-Ralphio's awfulness

jokes.text: The two lines I've put into every song I've ever sang, 'Spread your wings and fly,' and 'You deserve to be a champion.'
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his songwriting philosophy
jokes.punchline: Only has two generic inspirational lines he reuses in every song
jokes.explanation: Great Andy character detail - his 'songwriting' is just recycling two cliched lines. Perfect specificity.

jokes.text: Every award, from the smallest trophy to the Nobel Prize, is nothing more than... Is nothing more than a great way to honor someone.
jokes.setup: Ron starting to deliver Leslie's anti-award speech
jokes.punchline: Completely reverses course mid-sentence to be positive
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Ron can't bring himself to trash Leslie publicly. Great character growth moment.

jokes.text: So, I would like to present this year's Dottie English Whatever Award to the person who actually deserves it, Leslie Knope.
jokes.setup: Ron deciding to give the award away
jokes.punchline: Still can't get the award name right, calling it 'Dottie English Whatever'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ron's indifference to the award details, even while doing the right thing

jokes.text: I don't want it. Just take the damn thing. You deserve it. No, no. Really. No, Ron, you deserve it. Really, but I don't want it. But you won it. And you should win it.
jokes.setup: Ron and Leslie both trying to give the award to the other
jokes.punchline: Awkward back-and-forth where neither wants the award
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of awkwardness as both characters try to be gracious

jokes.text: Yeah, well, I'm investing. I'm like Warren Buffay.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining giving Tom his money
jokes.punchline: Calls Warren Buffett 'Warren Buffay' like the Friends character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy malapropism - mixing up the famous investor with Phoebe Buffay. Great character ignorance.

jokes.text: What are you doing here? I bought three shares. Thanks for the tip.
jokes.setup: Tom surprised to see Donna at the club
jokes.punchline: She invested more than him after his failed pitch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - Donna went around Tom to invest more heavily. Perfect economics and character payoff. Excellent structure.

tmdb_overview: Leslie is outraged when Ron wins a "Woman of the Year" award for a project she started. Meanwhile, Tom is desperate to find some money to invest in a local Pawnee club.
insight: Leslie's award episode sustains 2.56 jokes-per-minute without sacrificing character depth.
